fields.description: Which endpoint was called: check_label | register | deregister | list.
fields.label: Endpoint

fields.description: The label argument the call carried; BLANK for list() (which takes no argument).
fields.label: Label

fields.description: The SAME value the endpoint returned/threw: ok | taken | reserved | at_limit | invalid | unresolved. No synthetic codes. "unresolved" = caller resolution found no VM (a spoof attempt).
fields.label: Status

fields.description: 1 = a rules decline (taken/reserved/at_limit/invalid) or an unresolved source; 0 = a clean ok. A @rate_limit throttle is NOT a row here (it raises before the endpoint body runs).
fields.label: Business Reject

fields.description: The resolved VM name as a Data SNAPSHOT (NOT a Link): an audit row must survive the VM's deletion. BLANK for a spoof attempt (no VM resolved), paired with the spoofing source_ip.
fields.label: VM

fields.description: The /128 caller resolution KEYED ON (frappe.local.request_ip) — the exact value the trust decision used, recorded even when it resolved to no VM, so the spoofer's /128 is captured.
fields.label: Source IP

fields.description: The forwarded-header chain (incl. the raw X-Forwarded-For), guest-controlled, stored VERBATIM. When source_ip (a clean edge-supplied peer) and a guest-prepended X-Forwarded-For here DISAGREE, that divergence IS the leftmost-XFF forgery signal.
fields.label: Forwarded Headers

fields.description: The raw POST body, guest-controlled, stored VERBATIM.
fields.label: Request Body
